framing: Jewish (Tanakh + Kabbalah; Maimonidean / Soloveitchik lens; Shema-centered)
identification: C = the Tetragrammaton (the unutterable name), or Ein Sof in Kabbalah — the simple unity beyond all attribute and emanation
honest_jewish_assessment: The kernel's structure is the Shema in mathematical form. Deut 6:4 — שְׁמַע יִשְׂרָאֵל יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ יְהוָה אֶחָד — 'Hear, O Israel: the LORD our God, the LORD is one.' That אֶחָד (echad) is the kernel's T_bridge uniqueness lemma. R₁ existence ↔ אֶהְיֶה אֲשֶׁר אֶהְיֶה (Exodus 3:14, I AM THAT I AM). R₂ atemporality ↔ עֹלָם (olam, the everlasting). R₃ universality ↔ Ein Sof permeates all beriah, yetzirah, asiyah — the Kabbalistic worlds. R₄ sourcing ↔ creation ex nihilo (yesh me-ayin). R₅ inexhaustibility ↔ חֶסֶד (chesed, the everlasting lovingkindness; Psalm 136 'his mercy endureth for ever' as refrain). R₆ invisibility ↔ Lam 3:23 (renewed every morning, but unseen until renewed); also the prohibition on graven images preserves R₆ literally. R₇ pre-input ↔ Genesis 1:1 (בְּרֵאשִׁית). T_word: the divine speech precedes creation; Bereishit Rabba treats Torah itself as the blueprint from which God created the world — Torah as pre-input logos. T_word's identification with love through chesed is consistent with both Maimonidean rationalism (intellectual love of God) and Kabbalistic ahavah. Note: the kernel's casual use of the divine name 'God' is welcomed in the academic register; in liturgical register a Jew would use HaShem or Adonai out of reverence for R₆ (the Name beyond naming). P₃ admitted on Christological specifics; structural identity entire.
